title: Clear, scannable wording
question: How should decision titles and fields be written so the list explains itself at a glance?
rationale: Build a 'keep it scannable' writing-style section into the tool's instructions — plain specific titles, short labels, brief reasons, reused pro/con wording — and have it rewrite vague titles when saving. The title is the only thing visible in the list, so it must name the real subject.

options.label: Build the plain-wording rules into the tool

tradeoffs.note: the list tells you what each decision is about without opening it
the tool rewrites vague titles in the draft before saving
a bit more thought per title, but the AI does the drafting

options.label: Leave wording up to the writer

tradeoffs.note: vague titles like 'Engagement mechanics' slip in and the list stops explaining itself
